description: Define a canonical appliance status JSON model that includes release metadata, URLs, rollup state, readiness tiers, top blockers, components, and recent events.
Implement tiered readiness rollups for PLATFORM_READY, CORE_READY, BOOTSTRAP_READY, LOGIN_READY, BACKGROUND_READY, and FULLY_HEALTHY.
Classify user-facing rollup states as Installing, Ready to log in, Ready with background issues, Fully healthy, or Failed/action required.
Enhance the existing appliance status CLI to report the canonical status model and tiered readiness.
Enhance bootstrap CLI output to print phase-by-phase progress for Talos, Kubernetes, storage, Flux, core app, and background service installation.
Generate a bootstrap status token, persist it under the appliance config directory, and print it with the status URL during bootstrap.
Create an appliance-system namespace and in-cluster appliance-status-auth Secret containing the generated status token.
Add an early-installed appliance-status chart or manifest set that exposes a token-protected status service on a predictable node port, defaulting to 8080.
Implement a token-protected overview page that shows install state, current phase, login URL when available, and the highest-priority user action.
Implement a token-protected advanced diagnostics page showing readiness tiers, component status, top blockers, recent events, and Helm/Flux state.
Give appliance-status read-only Kubernetes RBAC for nodes, pods, jobs, PVCs, events, Flux resources, HelmReleases, and selected metadata-only ConfigMaps/Secrets.
Detect DNS resolver failures during Talos or Kubernetes bootstrap and surface explicit DNS remediation guidance.
Detect Postgres PVC/subPath initialization failures and classify them as login-blocking core storage blockers.
Detect image tag not found failures and classify them as login-blocking or background-only based on component tier.
Detect interrupted image pulls separately from missing image tags and report them as retryable image pull interruptions.
When Helm install times out, inspect underlying pods, jobs, and events so the top blocker reports the root cause instead of only the Helm timeout.
Detect bootstrap job states including waiting, running, failed, and completed, and summarize migration/seed success for BOOTSTRAP_READY.
Detect Temporal schema/autosetup failures and report schema initialization guidance.
Detect Kubernetes service-link environment collisions in Temporal UI or similar services and report disable-service-links guidance.
Split appliance Flux resources into platform, core, and background groups with explicit dependency order.
Ensure background Flux/Helm failures do not unset LOGIN_READY when core app readiness criteria are satisfied.
Validate release manifest image references before applying background releases and report missing tags as release artifact blockers.
Fix fresh reset helper behavior so it handles new or empty Talos appliance disks without an unbound variable failure.
Fix operator/script handling so explicit kubeconfig and talosconfig reuse does not regenerate or overwrite existing Talos credentials unexpectedly.
Update Temporal chart/runtime values so Temporal autosetup runs during install and service links are disabled where needed.
Make LOGIN_READY detection verify Alga web deployment readiness, app HTTP response, dashboard/login redirect, and representative seed data.
Persist and expose the selected appliance release version, app version, channel, and Git revision in the status model.
Add support-bundle entry point metadata to the advanced diagnostics model, with implementation of bundle download allowed in a later milestone.
